commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And of your garments you did take, and adorned your high places with various colors, and played the harlot on them: the like things have never been, neither shall be. Places: pavilions, (Cornelius a Lap ide; 4 Kings xvii. ; Calmet) or idols stuffed, (St. Jerome; Theodoret) and outwardly adorned. Such might easily be procured or removed, 1 Kings xix. Hereafter, with impunity. The Jews were guilty of greater ingratitude than other nations.
